spectrum.right: Let it go to avoid tension

header_0.cue: A coworker takes credit for your idea
header_0.raw_response: When a coworker takes credit for my idea, I find myself oscillating between wanting to confront the issue and choosing to let it go. On one hand, I value transparency and fairness, which makes me inclined to address the situation directly. On the other hand, I also understand the importance of maintaining a peaceful work environment and avoiding unnecessary tension. Given my tendency to consider the broader impact on team dynamics and my own well-being, I usually opt for letting it go to avoid escalating the situation and potentially damaging my relationships with colleagues. However, I also recognize that not confronting the issue can lead to a lack of accountability and a loss of recognition, which I find uncomfortable. Therefore, I would place this scenario closer to the middle of the spectrum, leaning slightly towards letting it go to avoid tension.
